_meta.description: Reference framework for translating climate impact into project economics (NPV/IRR/DSCR) and a Climate Risk Premium (CRP). Every threshold/spread carries a citable source; financing defaults flagged 'model assumption' are starting points the user overrides per project/asset. The CRP is a counterfactual: the increase in credit spread / WACC from the no-climate baseline to the climate-stressed cashflow.
_meta.provenance: DSCR→rating: selectable methodology (rating_methods) — default Moody's Global Infrastructure Finance (2021) + S&P Project Finance Criteria (2022), with indicative lender-conservative and sponsor-lenient variants and a user 'custom' grid. Rating→spread: Bloomberg US Corporate Bond Index 2020–2024 average (distressed levels for CC/C/D). Aligned with PLANiT crp-core so the two tools price consistently.
_meta.rating_methods_note: rating_dscr_thresholds is retained as the back-compat default and equals rating_methods.moodys_sp.thresholds.

moodys_sp.label: Moody's / S&P (infrastructure & project finance)
moodys_sp.source: Moody's Global Infrastructure Finance (2021) + S&P Project Finance Criteria (2022)
moodys_sp.note: Default. DSCR bands typical of rated infrastructure / project-finance debt.

lender_conservative.label: Lender / banking case (conservative)
lender_conservative.source: Indicative — stricter DSCR bands typical of project-finance lender base cases
lender_conservative.note: Higher coverage required for each rating; downgrades climate-stressed cashflows sooner.

equity_lenient.label: Sponsor / equity case (lenient)
equity_lenient.source: Indicative — lower DSCR bands typical of sponsor base cases
equity_lenient.note: Lower coverage tolerated for each rating; holds ratings under modest climate stress.

rating_spreads_bps.source: Bloomberg US Corp Bond Index 2020–2024 avg
